name: Pamela's PRO Workout
description: Compact programmable clock-synced modulation source with 8 independent outputs. Each output freely configurable as LFO, trigger, envelope, Euclidean pattern, or random. BPM 10–330, with CV inputs and cross-output modulation.

controls.name: Program Knob
controls.description: Push-turn encoder for all UI navigation. Twist to scroll outputs/values, push to select, push+hold to access extended parameters.
controls.detail: Push: select · Twist: adjust · Hold (>1s): extended params

controls.name: Start / Stop Button
controls.description: Starts and stops the master clock. Pressing stop also resets clock position.

inputs.name: CLK
inputs.description: External clock input. Overrides internal BPM when patched. Rising edge 700mV min.

inputs.name: RUN
inputs.description: Run/stop input. High voltage starts clock, low stops it. Can also act as CV 3 or Reset.

inputs.name: CV 1
inputs.description: Assignable CV input 1. Assignable to any output parameter with individual attenuation and offset.

inputs.name: CV 2
inputs.description: Assignable CV input 2. Assignable to any output parameter with individual attenuation and offset.

outputs.name: OUT 1–8
outputs.description: Eight independent clocked CV/gate outputs with LED indicators. Each fully programmable with shape, divisor/multiplier, Euclidean, probability, and cross-modulation.

behaviors.name: Per-Output Clock Division / Multiplication
behaviors.description: Each output has an independent multiplier or divisor from /16384 to ×192 including non-integer triplet and dotted values. Utility modes: GATE (held high), OFF, START pulse, STOP pulse.

behaviors.name: Waveform Shapes
behaviors.description: Each output selects from: Gate/Pulse, Ratchet ×2/×4, Triangle, Trapezoid, Sine, Hump, Exp/Log Envelope, Classic Random (S&H), Smooth Random. Width/Slew parameter shapes each waveform.

behaviors.name: Euclidean Patterns
behaviors.description: Generate Euclidean rhythms per output. Set Steps (total), Triggers (hits), Pad (empty suffix steps), and Shift (rotation). Pattern loops can reset non-4/4 patterns.

behaviors.name: Probability & Loops
behaviors.description: Probability sets % chance each step fires. Loop specifies reset point in beats. Loop Nap/Wake creates sleeping patterns (output off for N loops, active for M loops).

behaviors.name: Cross Operations
behaviors.description: 17 operations between outputs: AND, OR, S&H, MULT, MASK, SEED, FLIP, MIN, MAX, and more. Enables internal modulation routing without cables.

behaviors.name: FLEX Timing
behaviors.description: Micro-timing offsets: HUMAN (random drift), SWING (every-other delay), RAMP UP/DOWN (accelerating/decelerating), HUMP (arc tempo).

behaviors.name: Loop Wake/Nap
behaviors.description: Output plays for N loops (Wake), then silences for N loops (Nap). Creates call-and-response phrases and automatic fills without any external sequencer or logic.

behaviors.name: Quantiser
behaviors.description: Quantises output to musical scales. Custom scales saveable. Applied after cross operations.

behaviors.name: Triggered envelope mode
behaviors.description: Setting an output to Triggered type converts any waveform shape into a one-shot envelope that fires on each rising edge from a CV input. Pamela becomes an 8-channel clocked envelope generator — each channel can have a different shape (exp, log, sine, triangle) and length independent of the master BPM.

behaviors.name: Loop Shift for evolution
behaviors.description: Loop Shift advances the start point of a pattern by N steps each time the loop resets. The pattern slowly walks through a longer underlying sequence cycle by cycle, preventing static repetition over long periods. Combine with Euclidean patterns for sequences that feel generative without true randomness.

behaviors.name: RESEED for controlled randomness
behaviors.description: One output set to Cross Op SEED with Cross Src pointing to another output: the source output's rising edge resets the random seed of the SEED output, causing it to replay the same random sequence from that reset point. Provides deterministic re-rolls on demand — useful for generative patches that need repeatability at phrase boundaries.

patchIdeas.name: Euclidean clave
patchIdeas.patch: A 7-in-16 Euclidean pattern gives you the classic bell clave line with one output.
steps: Push the program knob on the BPM screen, twist to ~120 BPM, push to confirm.
Twist to the OUT 1 screen, push+hold the knob ~1s to enter extended parameters.
Twist to Eucl Steps, push, set to 16, push to save.
Twist to Eucl Trigs, push, set to 7, push to save.
Twist to Eucl Shift, push, set to 0, push (push+hold to exit back).
Patch OUT 1 → your drum's trigger input.
Press Start / Stop to run the clock.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — gate pulses, 0–5V, at the 7-in-16 Euclidean positions.
patchIdeas.hear: The familiar 3-2 clave pattern. Change Shift to 1 or 2 for a rotated Cuban feel; higher values scatter the accents.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Euclidean Steps=16 Triggers=7 Rotate=0

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Drum (Trig) // classic clave bell pattern

patchIdeas.name: Internal S&H sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: OUT 2's quarter-note gate samples OUT 1's smooth random onto a melody — no external S&H needed.
steps: On OUT 1 modifier, set divisor to x4 (fast free-running source).
OUT 1 extended: Shape = Smooth Random.
OUT 1 extended: Loop = 16 (beats to repeat the random seed).
OUT 1 extended: Cross Op = S&H, Cross Src = Out 2.
OUT 2 modifier: /4 (one gate per beat) — this is the S&H clock.
Patch OUT 1 → VCO 1V/oct.
Press Start / Stop. Quantise OUT 1 on its extended Quantiser screen to a scale you like.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — stepped pitch CV, 0–5V, updated on every OUT 2 gate.
patchIdeas.hear: A random melody that changes on every quarter note and repeats every 16 beats. Reseed via the Reset Seed shortcut to roll a new 16-beat phrase.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Smooth Random, Loop=16, Cross Op S&H, Cross Src OUT 2
* OUT 2: Gate /4

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) p> VCO (V/oct) // stepped random melody on quarter notes

patchIdeas.name: Polyrhythm with prime divisions
patchIdeas.patch: Three prime divisors on three outputs create a polyrhythm that only resolves after 1001 beats.
steps: OUT 1 modifier: /7. OUT 1 Shape = Gate, Width ~50%.
OUT 2 modifier: /11. OUT 2 Shape = Gate, Width ~50%.
OUT 3 modifier: /13. OUT 3 Shape = Gate, Width ~50%.
Patch OUT 1 → Drum 1 trigger.
Patch OUT 2 → Drum 2 trigger.
Patch OUT 3 → Drum 3 trigger.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1, OUT 2, OUT 3 — independent gates 0–5V at /7, /11, /13 of beat.
patchIdeas.hear: Three drums that never line up the same way twice within a set. A drifting generative feel with zero randomness — pure math.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate /7
* OUT 2: Gate /11
* OUT 3: Gate /13

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) g> Drum 1 (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) g> Drum 2 (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) g> Drum 3 (Trig) // LCM 1001 steps before repeat

patchIdeas.name: Gate masking for ghost notes
patchIdeas.patch: A dense Euclidean pattern is masked by a sparse one to produce syncopated ghost hits.
steps: OUT 1 extended: Eucl Steps=16, Trigs=13 (dense pattern).
OUT 2 extended: Eucl Steps=16, Trigs=5 (sparse gate).
OUT 1 extended: Cross Op = MASK, Cross Src = Out 2. OUT 1 only passes when OUT 2 is high.
Patch OUT 1 → percussion trigger.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — gate pulses, 0–5V, only when OUT 2 pattern is high.
patchIdeas.hear: A sparse stutter of hits where the dense and sparse patterns overlap — exactly the ghost-note feel of live drumming. Change either Eucl Trigs value to re-sculpt density.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Euclidean 16/13, Cross Op MASK, Cross Src OUT 2
* OUT 2: Euclidean 16/5

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Drum (Trig) // dense pattern masked to sparse ghost hits

patchIdeas.name: Boolean rhythm logic (AND)
patchIdeas.patch: OUT 3 is copied from OUT 2 and AND'd against OUT 1 — firing only where the two Euclidean patterns coincide.
steps: OUT 1 extended: Eucl Steps=16, Trigs=7, Shift=0.
OUT 2 extended: Eucl Steps=16, Trigs=5, Shift=3.
OUT 3 modifier: x1 (same grid resolution).
OUT 3 extended: Shape = Gate, Width ~25%.
OUT 3 extended: Cross Op = AND, Cross Src = Out 1. OUT 3 = (its own gate) AND OUT 1.
Also route OUT 2 via a second Cross path by stacking OUT 2 into OUT 3's CV input and assigning Eucl Trigs to CV — or simpler, set OUT 3 to copy OUT 2's pattern via Load/Save and AND against OUT 1.
Patch OUT 3 → percussion trigger.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 3 — gate pulses, 0–5V, only where OUT 1 and OUT 2 both fire.
patchIdeas.hear: A very sparse set of hits where both patterns align. Nudge OUT 2 Shift to walk the coincidences around — the pattern breathes without losing grid.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Euclidean 16/7 Rotate=0
* OUT 2: Euclidean 16/5 Rotate=3
* OUT 3: Gate /1, Cross Op AND, Cross Src OUT 1

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) t> Drum (Trig) // fires only where OUT 1 AND OUT 2 coincide

patchIdeas.name: Wake/Nap call-and-response
patchIdeas.patch: Loop Wake/Nap on two outputs makes one voice call for two bars then rest while the other answers.
steps: OUT 1 modifier: /4 (one step per beat). Shape = Smooth Random, Quantiser = a scale you like.
OUT 1 extended: Loop = 4 beats. Loop Wake = 2, Loop Nap = 1.
OUT 2 modifier: /4. Shape = Smooth Random, Quantiser = same scale, different note range via Offset.
OUT 2 extended: Loop = 4. Loop Wake = 1, Loop Nap = 2.
Patch OUT 1 → VCO 1 V/oct. Route a clock (separate output, or Pam's OUT 3 at /8) to VCO 1 envelope.
Patch OUT 2 → VCO 2 V/oct with its own envelope trigger.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 and OUT 2 — quantised pitch CV 0–5V, each active only during its Wake loops.
patchIdeas.hear: Voice 1 plays for two bars, rests one; voice 2 fills the silence. Breathing call-and-response with no manual muting.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Melody CV, Loop=4, Wake=2, Nap=1
* OUT 2: Melody CV, Loop=4, Wake=1, Nap=2

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) p> VCO 1 (V/oct) // call
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) p> VCO 2 (V/oct) // response in the gap

patchIdeas.name: Drum fill every 8 bars
patchIdeas.patch: A dense fill pattern is silenced for 7 bars via Loop Nap, then bursts on the 8th bar only.
steps: OUT 1 modifier: /4. Shape = Gate, Width ~30%. Continuous main kick trigger.
OUT 2 extended: Eucl Steps=16, Trigs=12. Dense fill pattern.
OUT 2 extended: Loop = 8 beats.
OUT 2 extended: Loop Wake = 1, Loop Nap = 7. Pattern runs for one 8-beat loop then sleeps for seven.
Patch OUT 1 → Kick trigger.
Patch OUT 2 → Fill drum trigger.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 2 — gate pulses, 0–5V, active only during the Wake loop.
patchIdeas.hear: Eight bars of just kick, then the final bar explodes into a dense tom fill. Repeats forever. Change Loop Nap for fill frequency.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Main drum trigger, continuous
* OUT 2: Euclidean 16/12, Loop=8, Wake=1, Nap=7

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) t> Fill Drum (Trig) // fires only on bar 8

patchIdeas.name: Smooth random portamento melody
patchIdeas.patch: Smooth Random into the Quantiser produces a gliding pentatonic melody without harsh jumps.
steps: OUT 1 modifier: x4 (steps are 1/4 beat — fast source for smooth glide).
OUT 1 extended: Shape = Smooth Random.
OUT 1 extended: Slew ~40% to round the corners further.
OUT 1 extended: Loop = 8 beats so the phrase repeats every two bars.
OUT 1 extended: Quantiser = Pentatonic (pick your root).
Patch OUT 1 → VCO 1V/oct.
Route another output at /4 as trigger to your envelope/VCA.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — quantised pitch CV, 0–5V with slew, steps every 1/4 beat.
patchIdeas.hear: A gliding melody that slides between pentatonic notes rather than snapping — like portamento on an analog mono synth. Reset Seed to re-roll the loop.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Smooth Random, rate x4, Loop=8, Quantize=pentatonic

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) p> VCO (V/oct) // gliding pentatonic melody

patchIdeas.name: Quadrature sine pair
patchIdeas.patch: Two sine LFOs at the same division, with 90° phase between them, drive circular stereo motion.
steps: OUT 1 modifier: /4. Extended: Shape = Sine.
OUT 1 extended: Phase = 0%.
OUT 2 modifier: /4. Extended: Shape = Sine.
OUT 2 extended: Phase = 25% (a quarter cycle = 90°).
Patch OUT 1 → Left VCA CV (or filter cutoff L).
Patch OUT 2 → Right VCA CV.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 and OUT 2 — sine CV, 0–5V (centered via Offset=50% if you want ±), quarter-phase offset.
patchIdeas.hear: Sound orbits the stereo field — as OUT 1 dips, OUT 2 rises. On a Lissajous scope the two trace a circle.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Sine LFO /4
* OUT 2: Sine LFO /4, Phase=90

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) >> VCA L (CV)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) >> VCA R (CV) // quadrature rotating stereo

patchIdeas.name: FLEX humanised hi-hat
patchIdeas.patch: FLEX HUMAN scatters micro-timing around the grid so a 16th-note hat feels played, not programmed.
steps: OUT 1 extended: Eucl Steps=16, Trigs=12.
OUT 1 extended: Shape = Gate, Width ~20% (tight hat pulse).
OUT 1 extended: FLEX Op = HUMAN.
OUT 1 extended: FLEX Amount = 15% (subtle drift).
Patch OUT 1 → closed hi-hat trigger.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — gate pulses, 0–5V, arriving slightly early or late on each step.
patchIdeas.hear: A hat pattern that feels loose and alive. Push FLEX Amount to 35–40% for an obvious drunk-drummer feel; 5–10% is enough to kill the stiff machine grid.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Euclidean 16/12, FLEX HUMAN 15%

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Hi-Hat (Trig) // micro-timing drift per hit

patchIdeas.name: Euclidean four-piece drum bed
patchIdeas.patch: Four Euclidean outputs with different trigger counts and shifts drive kick, snare, hat, perc from one module.
steps: OUT 1 extended: Eucl Steps=16, Trigs=3, Shift=0 (kick — downbeats).
OUT 2 extended: Eucl Steps=16, Trigs=5, Shift=2 (snare — backbeat-ish).
OUT 3 extended: Eucl Steps=16, Trigs=7, Shift=5 (hat — syncopated 16ths).
OUT 4 extended: Eucl Steps=16, Trigs=4, Shift=3 (perc — offbeat accents).
Patch OUT 1 → Kick, OUT 2 → Snare, OUT 3 → Hat, OUT 4 → Perc.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–4 — four independent gate trains, 0–5V, each at its Euclidean positions.
patchIdeas.hear: A full polyrhythmic drum bed from one clock source. Tweak any Shift to walk the pattern, any Trigs to change density — the bed evolves without losing the pulse.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Euclidean 16/3
* OUT 2: Euclidean 16/5 Rotate=2
* OUT 3: Euclidean 16/7 Rotate=5
* OUT 4: Euclidean 16/4 Rotate=3

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) t> Snare (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) t> Hat (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 4) t> Perc (Trig) // full four-piece drum bed

patchIdeas.name: Self-reseeding probability cascade
patchIdeas.patch: A slow output reseeds its own probability channels via Cross Op SEED, re-rolling which steps fire every phrase.
steps: Settings → Input: Run = RESET (so RUN input can still receive a trigger, but we'll use SEED internally).
OUT 1 modifier: /4. Shape = Gate. Extended: Probability = 100% (always fires).
OUT 2 modifier: /4. Shape = Gate. Extended: Probability = 75%.
OUT 3 modifier: /4. Shape = Gate. Extended: Probability = 50%.
OUT 4 modifier: /4. Shape = Gate. Extended: Probability = 25%.
OUT 8 modifier: /32. Shape = Trigger pulse — this is the phrase boundary.
For each of OUT 2, 3, 4: Cross Op = SEED, Cross Src = Out 8. Each rising edge of OUT 8 reseeds their RNG.
Patch OUT 1–4 → Kick, Snare, Hat, Perc.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–4 — gates 0–5V, each row with its own probability density.
patchIdeas.hear: A groove with a rock-solid kick and progressively sparser voices on the higher outputs. Every 32 beats the whole probability grid re-rolls — the pattern shifts without drifting.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate /4, p=100%
* OUT 2: Gate /4, p=75%, Cross Op SEED, Cross Src OUT 8
* OUT 3: Gate /4, p=50%, Cross Op SEED, Cross Src OUT 8
* OUT 4: Gate /4, p=25%, Cross Op SEED, Cross Src OUT 8
* OUT 8: Trig /32

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) t> Snare (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) t> Hat (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 4) t> Perc (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Tempo-synced pad swells
patchIdeas.patch: A slow sine opens the filter while a slower descending ramp closes the amp — BPM-locked pad motion.
steps: OUT 5 modifier: /16 (one cycle every 4 bars of 16ths, i.e. 1 bar). Shape = Sine.
OUT 5 extended: Phase = 25% (90°) so the peak lands on the bar backbeat.
OUT 6 modifier: /32 (2-bar cycle). Shape = Triangle with Width = 100% (saw-down).
Patch OUT 5 → VCF cutoff CV.
Patch OUT 6 → VCA level CV.
Press Start / Stop and play a sustained pad through the VCF/VCA chain.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 5 — sine CV 0–5V per bar; OUT 6 — descending ramp CV 0–5V per 2 bars.
patchIdeas.hear: A filter that blooms once per bar over an amp that fades across two bars. Two slow curves on different cycles = the breathing swell of a tempo-locked pad.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 5: Sine LFO /16, Phase=90
* OUT 6: Saw Down /32

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 5) >> VCF (Cutoff)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 6) >> VCA (Level) // tempo-synced pad swells

patchIdeas.name: Euclidean arpeggiator
patchIdeas.patch: OUT 1's Euclidean gate also samples OUT 2's random CV, giving you a gate and a new pitch together.
steps: OUT 1 modifier: x2. Extended: Eucl Steps=8, Trigs=5. Shape = Gate, Width ~30%.
OUT 2 modifier: x2. Extended: Shape = Classic Random.
OUT 2 extended: Quantiser = A minor.
OUT 2 extended: Cross Op = S&H, Cross Src = Out 1. New pitch samples only on each OUT 1 hit.
Patch OUT 1 → envelope trigger.
Patch OUT 2 → VCO 1V/oct.
Envelope out → VCA CV. VCO → VCA in.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — gate 0–5V at Euclidean positions; OUT 2 — quantised pitch CV 0–5V, stepped on each gate.
patchIdeas.hear: An arpeggio in A minor where every note lands on the Euclidean rhythm. Each note is a fresh random scale degree. Change Eucl Trigs for density; change Quantiser root for key.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Euclidean 8/5, Gate
* OUT 2: Classic Random, Cross Op S&H, Cross Src OUT 1, Quantiser A minor

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) g> Envelope (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) p> VCO (V/oct) // euclidean arp in A minor
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Polyrhythmic burst generator
patchIdeas.patch: Three prime divisions are OR'd together inside Pam's — dense percussion rolls from one output.
steps: OUT 1 modifier: x3. Shape = Gate, Width ~10%.
OUT 2 modifier: x5. Shape = Gate, Width ~10%.
OUT 3 modifier: x7. Shape = Gate, Width ~10%. Cross Op = OR, Cross Src = Out 1.
OUT 4 modifier: x1. Shape = Gate. Cross Op = OR, Cross Src = Out 2. Extended: Cross then OR with Out 3 via a second stage (or use Pam's own sum via the AXON-style stacking described in the manual).
Simpler route: cascade OR through sequential outputs — OUT 2 Cross OR Out 1; OUT 3 Cross OR Out 2. OUT 3 now represents the union.
OUT 3 extended: FLEX Op = SWING, Amount = 15% for groove.
Patch OUT 3 → percussion trigger.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 3 — merged gate train, 0–5V, at the union of the three prime divisions.
patchIdeas.hear: Dense, never-identical percussion rolls. The prime divisions guarantee the pattern doesn't repeat within a typical phrase.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate x3
* OUT 2: Gate x5, Cross Op OR, Cross Src OUT 1
* OUT 3: Gate x7, Cross Op OR, Cross Src OUT 2, FLEX SWING 15%

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) t> Percussion (Trig) // prime-division burst fills

patchIdeas.name: Eight-output generative brain
patchIdeas.patch: All eight outputs drive a full patch — clocks, gates, pitches, modulators, and scene changes from one module.
steps: OUT 1 modifier: x4. Shape = Gate. Clock to external sequencer.
OUT 2 modifier: /4. Shape = Gate, Width ~20%. Note trigger to envelope.
OUT 3 modifier: /4. Shape = Classic Random. Quantiser = C minor. Cross Op = S&H, Cross Src = Out 2. Pitch per note.
OUT 4 modifier: x2. Shape = Smooth Random. Slew ~60%. Timbre modulator.
OUT 5 modifier: /16. Shape = Sine. Filter LFO.
OUT 6 modifier: /32. Shape = Triangle Width 100% (saw-down). VCA swell.
OUT 7 modifier: /1. Extended: Eucl Steps=16, Trigs=2. Reverb send trigger.
OUT 8 modifier: /64. Shape = Trigger pulse. Scene/reset pulse.
Patch each output as listed in the diagram and press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–8 — clocks, gates, pitch CV, mod CV, and slow pulses, all 0–5V, all BPM-locked.
patchIdeas.hear: A self-contained evolving piece from a single patch: sequenced pitches, breathing filter, ducking amp, occasional reverb throws, and a scene change every 64 beats.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Clock x4
* OUT 2: Gate /4
* OUT 3: Random + S&H, Quantiser C minor
* OUT 4: Smooth Random x2
* OUT 5: Sine /16
* OUT 6: Saw-down /32
* OUT 7: Euclidean 16/2
* OUT 8: Trig /64

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) c> Sequencer (Clock)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) g> Envelope (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) p> VCO (V/oct)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 4) >> VCO (Timbre)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 5) >> VCF (Cutoff)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 6) >> VCA (Level)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 7) g> Reverb (Send Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 8) g> Sequencer (Reset) // full patch brain

patchIdeas.name: Probability-swapped hi-hats
patchIdeas.patch: Two 16th-note hats with inverse probabilities rarely overlap — natural closed/open variation.
steps: OUT 1 modifier: x4 (16th notes). Shape = Gate, Width ~15% (tight closed hat).
OUT 1 extended: Probability = 80%. FLEX Op = SWING, Amount = 8%.
OUT 2 modifier: x4. Shape = Gate, Width ~60% (longer for open hat).
OUT 2 extended: Probability = 20%. FLEX Op = SWING, Amount = 0%.
Patch OUT 1 → Closed hat trigger.
Patch OUT 2 → Open hat trigger.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — swung 80% closed-hat gates; OUT 2 — straight 20% open-hat gates. Both 0–5V.
patchIdeas.hear: A busy closed hat with occasional open-hat accents in the gaps — the classic dance-music hat ornament pattern, with no programming beyond two probabilities.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate x4, p=80%, FLEX SWING 8%
* OUT 2: Gate x4, p=20%, FLEX SWING 0%

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Closed Hat (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) t> Open Hat (Trig) // inverse probability variation

patchIdeas.name: Rotating FM brightness in stereo
patchIdeas.patch: Quadrature sines drive FM index and stereo pan — brightness peaks as the sound crosses center.
steps: OUT 5 modifier: /16. Shape = Sine. Phase = 0%. Level = 40% (gentle FM depth).
OUT 6 modifier: /16. Shape = Sine. Phase = 25% (90°). Level = 100%, Offset = 0% (full pan sweep).
Patch OUT 5 → VCO FM index CV.
Patch OUT 6 → stereo panner CV.
Press Start / Stop with a sustained note playing through the VCO and panner.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 5 — sine 0–2V for FM depth; OUT 6 — sine 0–5V for pan. 90° offset between them.
patchIdeas.hear: As the sound moves left, it brightens; crossing center, it peaks in harmonics; moving right, it darkens. Sound seems to physically orbit your head.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 5: Sine /16, Phase=0
* OUT 6: Sine /16, Phase=25% (90°)

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 5) >> VCO (FM Index)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 6) >> Panner (CV) // 90° offset brightness vs position

patchIdeas.name: CV-swept Euclidean rotation
patchIdeas.patch: An external slow LFO patched into CV 1 walks OUT 1's Euclidean Shift through all 16 rotations.
steps: OUT 1 extended: Eucl Steps=16, Trigs=5, Shift=0. Shape = Gate.
On the Eucl Shift screen, twist past the minimum value to reveal the CV assign row.
Set CV source = CV1. Attenuation = 99%, Offset = 0%.
Patch a slow LFO (≥1 cycle per 16 bars) into Pam's CV 1 input. Use 0–5V range.
Patch OUT 1 → drum trigger.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — gates 0–5V whose position on the 16-step grid walks over time.
patchIdeas.hear: The drum pattern slowly morphs — every few bars the accents land somewhere new. Speed up the LFO for chaotic mutation, slow it down for glacial drift.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Euclidean 16/5, Eucl Shift ← CV 1

- External LFO (Out) >> Pamela's PRO Workout (CV 1)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Drum (Trig) // CV sweeps through all 16 rotations

patchIdeas.name: Clock-divided ratcheting kick
patchIdeas.patch: A fast probabilistic ratchet output is OR'd with the main kick — sparse 32nd-note ratchets fill between downbeats.
steps: OUT 1 modifier: /4. Shape = Gate, Width ~25%. Probability = 100%. Main kick.
OUT 2 modifier: x8 (32nd notes). Shape = Gate, Width ~10%. Probability = 25%.
OUT 2 extended: Cross Op = OR, Cross Src = Out 1. OUT 2 merges its ratchets with the main kick.
Patch OUT 2 → Kick trigger. (Or route through an external OR gate if you prefer.)
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 2 — union of main /4 beats and ~25% of the 32nd-note positions, 0–5V.
patchIdeas.hear: A solid four-on-the-floor with sporadic high-speed ratchets between the main hits — techno-style drive without a rigid drill pattern.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate /4, p=100%
* OUT 2: Gate x8, p=25%, Cross Op OR, Cross Src OUT 1

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) t> Kick (Trig) // main beats with random ratchets

patchIdeas.name: Two-against-three counterpoint
patchIdeas.patch: Two voices on different divisions, same scale, drift in and out of phase — instant polymetric counterpoint.
steps: OUT 1 modifier: /4. Shape = Gate, Width ~30%. Voice A trigger.
OUT 2 modifier: /4. Shape = Classic Random. Quantiser = D dorian. Cross Op = S&H, Cross Src = Out 1. Voice A pitch.
OUT 2 extended: Offset = 0% (keeps low register around C3).
OUT 3 modifier: /6. Shape = Gate, Width ~30%. Voice B trigger.
OUT 4 modifier: /6. Shape = Classic Random. Quantiser = D dorian. Cross Op = S&H, Cross Src = Out 3.
OUT 4 extended: Offset = 40% (shifts Voice B up about two octaves).
Patch OUT 1 → Env A trig, OUT 2 → VCO A 1V/oct.
Patch OUT 3 → Env B trig, OUT 4 → VCO B 1V/oct.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 & OUT 3 — gates 0–5V; OUT 2 & OUT 4 — quantised pitch CV 0–5V, two voices in D dorian.
patchIdeas.hear: Two voices on 4 and 6 that converge on downbeats and diverge between them. Same scale = they always sound consonant even as the phrasing drifts.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate /4
* OUT 2: Random /4 + S&H Src OUT 1, Quantiser D dorian
* OUT 3: Gate /6
* OUT 4: Random /6 + S&H Src OUT 3, Quantiser D dorian, Offset +2 oct

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) g> Env A (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) p> VCO A (V/oct)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) g> Env B (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 4) p> VCO B (V/oct) // 4-against-6 counterpoint

patchIdeas.name: Phrase-end scene trigger
patchIdeas.patch: One rare pulse at the end of every 64-beat phrase fans out to reset multiple modules at once.
steps: OUT 8 extended: Eucl Steps=64, Trigs=1, Shift=63 (one pulse on step 64 only).
OUT 8 extended: Shape = Gate, Width ~5% (short 20 ms pulse).
Patch OUT 8 → Marbles t-Reset.
Stack OUT 8 → Stages Reset.
Stack OUT 8 → Filter Bank Scene+ advance.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 8 — single short pulse, 0–5V, on beat 64 of every 64-beat phrase.
patchIdeas.hear: Silent for 63 beats, then every downstream module resets or advances in lockstep — a whole song moves to the next section on its own.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 8: Euclidean 64/1 Shift=63, Width=5%

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 8) g> Marbles (t Reset)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 8) g> Stages (Reset)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 8) g> Filter Bank (Scene+) // end-of-phrase scene advance

patchIdeas.name: Hub-and-spoke rhythm section
patchIdeas.patch: Three trigger outputs fire three envelopes on three VCAs while a slow sine animates the mix bus.
steps: OUT 1 modifier: /4. Shape = Gate, Width ~25%. Kick trigger.
OUT 2 modifier: x4. Shape = Gate, Width ~15%. Probability = 60%. Hat trigger.
OUT 3 extended: Eucl Steps=16, Trigs=5. Shape = Gate, Width ~20%. Perc trigger.
OUT 4 modifier: /32 (2-bar cycle). Shape = Sine. Mixer group level animator.
Patch OUT 1 → Env 1 trig; Env 1 → VCA Kick CV; Kick VCO → VCA Kick → Mixer 1.
Patch OUT 2 → Env 2 trig; Env 2 → VCA Hat CV; Hat noise → VCA Hat → Mixer 2.
Patch OUT 3 → Env 3 trig; Env 3 → VCA Perc CV; Perc source → VCA Perc → Mixer 3.
Patch OUT 4 → Mixer group level CV.
Press Start / Stop.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1/2/3 — gates 0–5V per voice; OUT 4 — sine CV 0–5V for bus dynamics.
patchIdeas.hear: A full rhythm section with kick, sparse hat, euclidean perc, and a slow swell that breathes across every two bars. One module, one clock, full groove.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Pamela's PRO Workout:
* OUT 1: Gate /4
* OUT 2: Gate x4, p=60%
* OUT 3: Euclidean 16/5
* OUT 4: Sine LFO /32

- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 1) t> Env 1 (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 2) t> Env 2 (Trig)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 3) t> Env 3 (Trig)
- Env 1 (Out) >> VCA Kick (CV)
- Env 2 (Out) >> VCA Hat (CV)
- Env 3 (Out) >> VCA Perc (CV)
- Kick VCO (Out) -> VCA Kick (In) -> Mixer (In 1)
- Hat Noise (Out) -> VCA Hat (In) -> Mixer (In 2)
- Perc Src (Out) -> VCA Perc (In) -> Mixer (In 3)
- Pamela's PRO Workout (OUT 4) >> Mixer (Level CV) // breathing dynamics
